_meta.description: Approved LLM output schema. Do not change without PM sign-off (Lucas).

changelog.2026-04-29: Replaced opportunity_score with three independent scores: odi_score, evidence_robustness, priority_score. All three are computed by odi_scorer.py and injected post-parse — never LLM-generated. PM sign-off: Lucas.
changelog.2026-05-13: Added job_type field (functional | emotional | social) to each opportunity. LLM-generated — not injected. PM sign-off: Lucas (assigned task).
changelog.2026-05-14: Added jtbd_confidence (high | medium | low) and jtbd_confidence_reason (one sentence) to each opportunity. LLM-generated. Clusters with <= 3 chunks get deterministic override to low post-parse. PM sign-off: Lucas (assigned task).

rules.json_only: Return ONLY valid JSON. No preamble. No markdown fences.
rules.jtbd_format: When I [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome].
rules.job_type_classification: functional = user wants to accomplish a task. emotional = user wants to feel a certain way or avoid a feeling. social = user wants to be perceived in a certain way. Default to functional.
rules.jtbd_confidence_rubric: high = all three JTBD parts directly evidenced by quotes. medium = mostly supported but at least one part required inference. low = cluster lacks a clear triggering situation or you abstracted significantly beyond the quotes.
rules.odi_fields: Do not generate odi_score, evidence_robustness, priority_score, importance, satisfaction, or source_type_diversity — these are all computed by odi_scorer.py and injected post-parse by llm.py on cluster_id.

score_definitions.odi_score: Classic ODI unmet-need signal. importance × (1 - satisfaction). Range 0–1. High = large cluster that is poorly satisfied.
score_definitions.evidence_robustness: Cross-source corroboration signal. (source_type_diversity × 0.65) + (normalised_cluster_size × 0.35). Range 0–1. High = theme appears across many source types.
score_definitions.priority_score: Synthesis of both signals. (odi_score × 0.60) + (evidence_robustness × 0.40). Range 0–1. Primary sort key for the results page.

example_output.goal: Increase 30-day retention for new users on the mobile app

opportunities.jtbd: When I first open the app after signing up, I want to understand what I can do with it, so I can decide if it is worth my time.
opportunities.jtbd_confidence_reason: All three JTBD parts are directly evidenced by quotes describing confusion on first open and abandonment after the first session.

assumptions.text: New users do not understand the core value proposition from the home screen alone.
An interactive walkthrough will not feel annoying if it can be skipped.
Empty states are a key drop-off point in the first session.
